fix: snapshot initial document before initActions mutate it

React SyncClient captures the initial document at construction time
(before initActions runs). This snapshot is passed to BaseSyncClient
so the hash and document sent to the server match. Previously the
BaseSyncClient called materialize() during connect(), which happened
AFTER initActions mutated the Denicek, causing a hash mismatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: migrate hot-path filesystem I/O from sync to async

Convert listSessionsFromDisk, readMessagesFromDisk, and hasPlan
checks from sync (readFileSync/statSync/existsSync) to async
(readFile/stat from fs/promises + Promise.all). Eliminates ~204ms
of event loop blocking per cache miss, improving concurrency
during active AI sessions.

commit.message:
fix(eval): add OIDC token refresh for long-running evaluations

The ai-agent-evals action can poll for 45-60+ minutes, exceeding the
Azure CLI access token lifetime. This causes AADSTS700024 errors when
the token can no longer be refreshed from the expired OIDC assertion.

Fix: create a continuously-refreshed federated token file (every 4 min)
and set AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE so DefaultAzureCredential uses
WorkloadIdentityCredential instead of AzureCliCredential. This allows
on-demand token renewal throughout the evaluation polling phase.

Applied to both evaluate-quality and evaluate-safety jobs. Added
continue-on-error with portal fallback reporting as a safety net.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* TypeForward Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions

Move all Azure.Identity source code into Azure.Core/src/Identity/ and convert
Azure.Identity into a TypeForwardedTo facade. This enables SDK libraries to
call WithAzureCredential() through their existing Azure.Core dependency
without adding a direct dependency on Azure.Identity.

Resolves #57184

commit.message:
refactor(drivers): replace bespoke per-runtime parsers with ACP base driver (#36)

* refactor(drivers): replace bespoke per-runtime parsers with ACP base driver

Introduce AcpRuntime trait and AcpDriver<R> as the unified transport
layer for all agent runtimes. The Agent Client Protocol (ACP) is a
JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio standard that all major coding agents support.

Architecture:
- AcpDriver<R: AcpRuntime> implements the Driver trait with shared
  ACP protocol handling: JSON-RPC lifecycle, session/update parsing,
  session/prompt encoding, prefix-agnostic tool display names
- AcpRuntime trait provides per-runtime metadata: binary name, CLI
  args, MCP bridge config, auth detection, model listing
- Each runtime file (claude.rs, codex.rs, kimi.rs, opencode.rs)
  shrinks from ~400-500 lines to ~50-80 lines
- Type aliases (ClaudeDriver = AcpDriver<ClaudeAcpRuntime>) keep
  existing references working

What goes away:
- 4 separate stdout JSON parsers (replaced by unified ACP parser)
- 4 separate stdin notification encoders (replaced by session/prompt)
- 4 separate tool_display_name/summarize_tool_input implementations
  (replaced by prefix-agnostic shared implementation)
- Per-runtime session flag plumbing in manager.rs

Net: -1894 lines removed, +213 lines added (including 14 new ACP
unit tests covering the full protocol pipeline).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01U2gKG3Tw8Vb1oebYKgXHAz

* feat(drivers): dual raw/ACP driver architecture with adapter auto-detection

Keep both raw (1.0) and ACP (2.0) drivers coexisting. The raw drivers
are the original bespoke per-runtime parsers; the ACP drivers use the
shared AcpDriver<R> protocol handler.

Selection logic in driver_for_runtime():
- If ACP adapter binary is on $PATH → use ACP driver
- Otherwise → fall back to raw driver

ACP adapter binaries per runtime:
- Claude: claude-agent-acp (via acpx/claude-agent-acp)
- Codex: codex-acp (via acpx/codex-acp)
- Kimi: kimi (native, subcommand `kimi acp`)
- OpenCode: opencode (native, subcommand `opencode acp`)

Files added:
- claude_raw.rs, codex_raw.rs, kimi_raw.rs, opencode_raw.rs
  (restored original raw drivers with *RawDriver struct names)

Files modified:
- claude.rs, codex.rs, kimi.rs, opencode.rs
  (fixed ACP binary names to correct adapter commands)
- mod.rs (driver_for_runtime() with ACP/raw fallback)
- manager.rs (restored Kimi session pre-write for raw driver)
- driver_tests.rs (8 tests: 4 raw + 4 ACP prompt tests)

194 total tests passing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01U2gKG3Tw8Vb1oebYKgXHAz

* fix(drivers): remove duplicate --input-format stream-json arg from claude raw driver

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(drivers): restore --input-format stream-json and fix notify_agent startup race

- Restore --input-format stream-json to claude_raw driver spawn args.
  This flag controls how claude reads stdin. Without it, the JSON startup
  message is treated as plain text and the agent never emits SessionInit.
  The flag was incorrectly removed as a "duplicate" of --output-format.

- Fix notify_agent startup race: when a DM arrives while an agent is still
  initializing (session_id is None), silently dropping the notification
  caused the message to be permanently lost. Now the pending count is
  incremented so the notification fires via stdin as soon as SessionInit
  is received.

- Remove stale teams: field references from PromptOptions/AgentConfig
  construction in all raw drivers and acp driver (compile errors after
  PR #35 removed the teams field).

- Add QA case DM-002: runtime-parameterized e2e spec for single-agent DM
  reply, controlled by CHORUS_RUNTIME and CHORUS_MODEL env vars.
  All 7 steps pass (17s end-to-end with claude/sonnet).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(acp): correct session/request_permission response format and remove stale retry logic

The ACP AllowedOutcome schema requires a nested outcome object:
  {"outcome": {"outcome": "selected", "optionId": "approve_for_session"}}

Our previous response sent the flat {"optionId": "..."} format which failed
Pydantic validation in kimi's _handle_approval_request, triggering the
except-Exception path that resolved the request as "reject". This caused
an infinite permission loop because approve_for_session was never stored
in ApprovalState.auto_approve_actions.

Also remove the retry-prompt logic: kimi awaits the permission response
inline within the same session/prompt call, so no follow-up prompt is needed.
Once approval is correctly processed, kimi continues the tool execution
and completes the turn normally.

Also drop --yolo from kimi spawn args: the kimi CLI callback discards all
flags when a subcommand is invoked, so --yolo has no effect in ACP mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(acp): add ACP driver SOP for failure diagnosis and new integration

Covers:
- Phase-based failure identification (init/session/prompt/permission)
- Raw wire capture and schema validation against ACP Pydantic models
- Silent failure path detection (timing-based, <200ms = exception)
- CLI subcommand flag gotcha (flags before subcommand are dropped)
- Permission inline-await behavior (no retry needed)
- Step-by-step new ACP driver integration checklist
- Quick reference: protocol flow diagram and permission response format

Informed by kimi ACP debugging: the root cause was a malformed
session/request_permission response that failed Pydantic validation
silently, causing an infinite permission loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(acp): restructure SOP with pre-code gates before Part 2

Reorganize so new driver integration (Part 1) comes first with three
mandatory pre-implementation gates:
- Gate 1: capture reference wire trace before writing any Rust
- Gate 2: read agent CLI source to verify flags aren't dropped by subcommand
- Gate 3: validate message formats via ACP Pydantic schema before implementing

Move CLI flag check from diagnostic (Part 2) to Gate 2 in Part 1 — it
is a build-time mistake, not a runtime one. Old Part 2 (adding a driver)
is replaced by the new Part 1 with explicit checkpoints. Diagnosis steps
remain as Part 2 for when things break despite following the gates.

commit.message:
Use audiomxd for Teams/EdgeTeams start detection, not physical mic

The previous approach (MSTeams running + physical mic active) fired whenever
Handy dictation or any other app opened the mic while Teams was running idle.

Now detect_teams_meeting() and detect_edge_teams_meeting() both use
_audiomxd_session_active(..., default_if_no_entries=False), which checks
whether Teams/Edge itself has logged an active recording session in the
last 120s.  Handy, audio recorders, and other apps don't generate MSTeams
or Microsoft Edge audiomxd entries, so they're invisible to this check.

The debounce (MEETING_CONFIRM_POLLS=3, 15s) is kept as a safety net for
any remaining transient probes Teams makes during call setup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(sync): replace fluent-ffmpeg with execFile in embedCoverArt + updateFileTags

Same root cause as qobuz.ts: fluent-ffmpeg's .outputOptions([array]) joins
elements with spaces then re-splits them, breaking any metadata value that
contains a space (title, artist, album, genre, 'Cover (front)', etc.).

Replaced both syncEngine methods with direct child_process.execFile() calls
using explicit string[] args arrays. Abort signal handling preserved via
proc.kill('SIGKILL'). getFfmpegBin() helper prefers FFMPEG_PATH env (set by
Electron), falls back to ffmpeg-static, then system ffmpeg.

Affected scenarios:
- Subsonic→Device sync: cover art embedding (all formats)
- Subsonic→Device sync: tag update step (all tracks with spaces in metadata)

Not affected: transcoder.ts (no metadata values written, only -map_metadata 0)

Extend debounce to all meeting types, not just EdgeTeams

mic probe) was still firing immediately, causing today's false positive.

(Zoom, Teams, EdgeTeams). The debounce tracks the current candidate app
and resets if it changes, preventing cross-app count carry-over.

fix(colony-subscriber): preserve manually-registered local branches during sweep

Colony sync was calling storage.unregister() on any local active branch
not present in the colony in-progress set — including branches registered
manually with `spidersan register`. This caused pulse to always report
the current branch as unregistered immediately after syncing.

Fix: only sweep branches that originated from colony sync, identified by
description starting with 'Colony claim by' or agent starting with 'agent:'.
Manually-registered local branches are left untouched.

fix: route AdPlug and V2M audio through ToneEngine master mixer

WASM streaming players (AdPlug, V2M) were connecting directly to
AudioContext.destination, bypassing Tone.Destination. This meant:
- Audio played through speakers but was invisible to AudioDataBus analyser
- get_audio_level MCP tool reported silence for all AdPlug/V2M playback
- Master volume/effects didn't apply to these players

Now route through ToneEngine.masterEffectsInput so audio goes through
the full mixer chain and is measurable by the analyser.
